An air pump must never run against fully: An air pump must never run against fully closed valves (dead-heading) — it overheats and can be damaged.
Anti short-cycle: once OFF, the pump sta: Anti short-cycle: once OFF, the pump stays off at least this long before it may restart. 0 = no limit. (An emergency stop always bypasses this.)
Anti short-cycle: once ON, the pump stay: Anti short-cycle: once ON, the pump stays on at least this long before it may be switched off (protects the motor). 0 = no limit.
Buttons are only available on aeration-v: Buttons are only available on aeration-valve channels — this channel drives the pump or the emergency valve.
Click on the map or drag the marker to s: Click on the map or drag the marker to set the location.
Dry-run (log only, do not switch hardwar: Dry-run (log only, do not switch hardware)
Force aeration on while the oxygen is be: Force aeration on while the oxygen is below the low threshold, until it recovers to the target.
Frost protection needs air-temperature m: Frost protection needs air-temperature monitoring (Sensors tab).
Geocoding failed: instance must be runni: Geocoding failed: instance must be running
Group aeration points to control them to: Group aeration points to control them together. There can never be more groups than points.
How long the motor valve needs to travel: How long the motor valve needs to travel fully open/closed. The safety logic waits this long instead of assuming an instant reaction.
How often the interlock is re-checked (s: How often the interlock is re-checked (seconds). Smaller = reacts faster to a dangerous state, but a little more load. 5 s is a good default.
If the adapter connection drops, the ESP: If the adapter connection drops, the ESP32 keeps running your time schedules on its own using its NTP clock — the dead-head safety interlock still applies. The cyclic sequence is not run autonomously; it stays with the adapter.
In dry-run the adapter only logs the int: In dry-run the adapter only logs the intended valve and pump actions — no hardware is switched.
Keep an ice-free hole open during the co: Keep an ice-free hole open during the cold season by forcing the selected points on.
Min. open valves while pump runs: Min. open valves while pump runs
Name: Name
No event selected — nothing will be sent: No event selected — nothing will be sent.
No response from the adapter. Is the ins: No response from the adapter. Is the instance running?
On (recommended): the valve is open with: On (recommended): the valve is open without power (fail-safe) — on a power cut the pump can always vent. Off: it is closed without power.
On: the adapter may switch the pump off: On: the adapter may switch the pump off (needs the pump state below). Off: the pump is only observed — the adapter never switches it, and relies on the emergency valve.
Only day and month are used (recurring e: Only day and month are used (recurring every year).
Only when it is cold (frost protection): Only when it is cold (frost protection)
Optional ordered cycle over points and/o: Optional ordered cycle over points and/or groups (mixed). Leave empty to rotate through all points.
Override button: an optional physical pu: Override button: an optional physical push-button (e.g. an ESP32 digital input or any boolean state). It works as a toggle — one press forces the point on with priority over the automatic control; only the master switch or a safety trip overrides it. Press again to release. Buttons are only available for aeration valves — a point that sits on the ESP32 pump or emergency-valve relay channel cannot have one.
Solenoid = opens/closes almost instantly: Solenoid = opens/closes almost instantly. Motorized ball valve (e.g. CWX-15N) needs a travel time and does not spring open on power loss — the dead-head protection then relies on the pump also losing power.
Tell the adapter about your air pump. If: Tell the adapter about your air pump. If it is controllable, the interlock may switch it off in an emergency; if it is only observed, the emergency valve alone protects it.
The aeration-point valves use the relay: The aeration-point valves use the relay channel set per point; here you map the emergency valve and the pump. The firmware runs an on-device failsafe from these settings.
The coordinates from the ioBroker system: The coordinates from the ioBroker system settings are used.
The ioBroker state that opens/closes the: The ioBroker state that opens/closes the emergency valve.
The ioBroker state that reports (and, if: The ioBroker state that reports (and, if controllable, switches) the pump. Leave empty if you have no pump signal.
The offset should be at least the averag: The offset should be at least the average time the animals need to eat.
The relief valve the interlock opens whe: The relief valve the interlock opens when too few normal valves are open. Wire it normally-open (NO) so it opens by itself on a power cut.
There are more groups than points.
These settings protect the pump and the: These settings protect the pump and the animals. Read the notes and test on your own hardware before unattended use.
Tick the events that should send a messa: Tick the events that should send a message to the selected instance. A short, localized text is sent on each edge (raise and clear).
Up to 8 aeration points. Each valve is a: Up to 8 aeration points. Each valve is an existing ioBroker state or an ESP32 channel.
When switching from one point to the nex: When switching from one point to the next, the new valve opens this many seconds before the old one closes (make-before-break), so there is never a moment with everything shut. 0 disables the overlap.
While the pump runs, at least this many: While the pump runs, at least this many valves are always kept open. If that cannot be reached, the emergency valve opens and the pump is switched off. Higher = safer against dead-heading, but forces more points open.
when dissolved oxygen drops too low or r: when dissolved oxygen drops too low or recovers
when the dead-head interlock trips or cl: when the dead-head interlock trips or clears
when the pressure leaves or re-enters it: when the pressure leaves or re-enters its range
